attributes.action: Cap yearly increases in rent, social housing rent and council tax
attributes.additional_details: I believe increases should be capped, linked to wage growth, or subject to an affordability test before approval. Housing is a basic necessity, and stronger safeguards are needed to prevent households being priced out of secure and stable living.
attributes.background: Many people face yearly increases in rent, social housing rent and council tax while wages do not keep pace. These essential costs are unavoidable and rising housing expenses are causing financial hardship and insecurity for households across the UK.

rejection.details:
The following petitions call for similar actions: 

Increase the Local Housing Allowance and freeze private rents
https://petition.parliament.uk/petitions/756698

Implement a rent cap on private rents matching the LHA to reduce overcrowding 
https://petition.parliament.uk/petitions/756107

Limit council tax increases to the lowest of either inflation or 5% 
https://petition.parliament.uk/petitions/753921
